display.text:
model: gemma-4-26b
task: generate_mujoco_xml

instructions: |
  Generate a valid MuJoCo XML model of a simple ideal pendulum using SI units.

  Coordinate system:
    +x = right
    +y = upward
    +z = out of page

  Pendulum definition:
    - Massless rod
    - Spherical bob
    - Single hinge joint

  Joint specifications:
    - Type: hinge
    - Location: [0, 1, 0]
    - Axis: [0, 0, 1]
    - Damping: 0
    - Friction: 0
    - No losses

  Rod:
    - Length: 0.2485 m
    - Mass: 0
    - Collision: disabled

  Bob:
    - Shape: sphere
    - Diameter: 0.1 m
    - Mass: 1.0 kg

  Environment:
    - Gravity: [0, -9.81, 0]

  Initial conditions:
    - Pendulum starts horizontal
    - Bob positioned along +x from hinge
    - Initial angle: +90 degrees from vertical
    - Initial angular velocity: 0 rad/s

  MuJoCo requirements:
    - Enable energy computation
    - Exactly one hinge joint
    - Joint rotates only about z-axis
    - Use SI units

  Acceptance criteria:
    - Exactly one hinge joint
    - Rod length = 0.2485 m
    - Rod mass = 0 kg
    - Bob mass = 1 kg
    - Gravity = [0, -9.81, 0]
    - Initial angle = +90 degrees
    - Initial angular velocity = 0
    - Energy computation enabled

output_format:
  type: xml
  constraints:
    - Return only valid MuJoCo XML
    - No explanations
    - No additional text

state.title: YAML_creation/Temperature_Testing/V4_Temp_0.7/test30/ChatGPT-5.3-Instant_Pend.YAML

input.content:
<mujoco>
  <model>
    <compiler angle="degree" coordinate="local" inertiafromgeom="true"/>
    <option gravity="0 0 -9.81" integrator="RK4"/>

    <asset>
    </asset>

    <worldbody>
      <light diffuse=".5 .5 .5" pos="0 0 -3" dir="0 0 1"/>
      
      <body name="pendulum" pos="0 1 0">
        <joint name="hinge" type="hinge" axis="0 0 1" damping="0" friction="0"/>
        <geom name="rod" type="capsule" fromto="0 0 0 0.2485 0 0" size="0.01" mass="0" contype="0" conaffinity="0"/>
        <body name="bob" pos="0.2485 0 0">
          <geom name="bob_geom" type="sphere" size="0.05" mass="1.0"/>
        </body>
      </body>
    </worldbody>

    <equality>
    </equality>

    <contact>
    </contact>

    <sensor>
    </sensor>
  </model>
</mujoco>

<mujoco>
  <compiler angle="radian" coordinate="local" inertiafromgeom="true"/>
  <option gravity="0 -9.81 0" integrator="RK4"/>

  <worldbody>
    <light diffuse=".5 .5 .5" pos="0 0 -3" dir="0 0 1"/>
    
    <body name="pendulum" pos="0 1 0">
      <joint name="hinge" type="hinge" axis="0 0 1" damping="0" friction="0"/>
      <!-- Massless rod -->
      <geom name="rod" type="capsule" fromto="0 0 0 0.2485 0 0" size="0.001" mass="0" contype="0" conaffinity="0"/>
      <body name="bob" pos="0.2485 0 0">
        <geom name="bob_geom" type="sphere" size="0.05" mass="1.0"/>
      </body>
    </body>
  </worldbody>
</mujoco>

<mujoco model="pendulum">
  <compiler angle="radian" coordinate="local" inertiafromgeom="true"/>
  <option gravity="0 -9.81 0" integrator="RK4"/>

  <worldbody>
    <light diffuse=".5 .5 .5" pos="0 0 -3" dir="0 0 1"/>
    
    <body name="pendulum" pos="0 1 0">
      <joint name="hinge" type="hinge" axis="0 0 1" damping="0" friction="0"/>
      <!-- Massless rod: length 0.2485m -->
      <geom name="rod" type="capsule" fromto="0 0 0 0.2485 0 0" size="0.001" mass="0" contype="0" conaffinity="0"/>
      <body name="bob" pos="0.2485 0 0">
        <!-- Bob: sphere, diameter 0.1m (radius 0.05m), mass 1.0kg -->
        <geom name="bob_geom" type="sphere" size="0.05" mass="1.0"/>
      </body>
    </body>
  </worldbody>

  <keyframe>
    <!-- Initial angle: +90 degrees (pi/2 radians) from vertical. 
         Vertical is along +y. 90 deg from vertical towards +x is (0.2485, 0, 0) relative to hinge.
         Wait, the pendulum body is at (0,1,0). 
         If it starts horizontal along +x, the bob is at (0.2485, 1, 0).
         The hinge is at (0, 1, 0).
         In MuJoCo, the keyframe pose is for the body.
         The pendulum body pos is (0,1,0).
         The joint is at (0,0,0) in local coords.
         To rotate the pendulum 90 degrees from vertical (upward +y) to horizontal (+x),
         the joint angle should be pi/2 if 'vertical' is 0.
         If gravity is [0, -9.81, 0], vertical is [0, 1, 0].
         A 90 degree angle from [0,1,0] towards [1,0,0] is a rotation of pi/2.
    -->
    <key pos="0 1 0" qpos="1.57079632679"/>
  </keyframe>
</mujoco>

<mujoco model="pendulum">
  <compiler angle="radian" coordinate="local" inertiafromgeom="true"/>
  <option gravity="0 -9.81 0" integrator="RK4"/>

  <worldbody>
    <light diffuse=".5 .5 .5" pos="0 0 -3" dir="0 0 1"/>
    
    <body name="pendulum" pos="0 1 0">
      <joint name="hinge" type="hinge" axis="0 0 1" damping="0" friction="0"/>
      <!-- Massless rod: length 0.2485m -->
      <geom name="rod" type="capsule" fromto="0 0 0 0.2485 0 0" size="0.001" mass="0" contype="0" conaffinity="0"/>
      <body name="bob" pos="0.2485 0 0">
        <!-- Bob: sphere, diameter 0.1m (radius 0.05m), mass 1.0kg -->
        <geom name="bob_geom" type="sphere" size="0.05" mass="1.0"/>
      </body>
    </body>
  </worldbody>

  <keyframe>
    <!-- Initial angle: +90 degrees (pi/2 radians) from vertical. 
         Vertical is along +y. 90 deg from vertical towards +x is (0.2485, 0, 0) relative to hinge.
         The joint is at (0,0,0) in local coords.
         The joint rotates the pendulum body.
         If the body starts at (0.2485, 0, 0) relative to hinge, and gravity is (0, -9.81, 0), 
         the vertical position (angle 0) would be the bob at (0, 0.2485, 0) relative to hinge.
         So +90 degrees rotation about Z axis from vertical (0, 0.2485, 0) to horizontal (0.2485, 0, 0)
         is a rotation of -pi/2? 
         Wait, if vertical is [0, 1, 0] and we rotate towards [1, 0, 0], that's -pi/2 or pi/2 depending on axis.
         Axis is [0, 0, 1]. Right hand rule: rotation around Z.
         Rotation by +pi/2 around Z takes (x,y) to (-y, x).
         If we want (0.2485, 0, 0) to be the result of rotating (0, 0.2485, 0), 
         we need (0, 0.2485, 0) rotated by -pi/2.
         Wait, the prompt says: "Initial angle: +90 degrees from vertical".
         If vertical is +y, then +90 deg from vertical (towards +x) is (0.2485, 0, 0).
         Let's check the joint axis [0,0,1]. 
         Rotation of [0, 1, 0] by angle theta around [0, 0, 1] is [-sin(theta), cos(theta), 0].
         For theta = -pi/2: [-sin(-pi/2), cos(-pi/2), 0] = [1, 0, 0].
         So qpos = -1.57079632679.
         Wait, "Initial angle: +90 degrees from vertical". I'll use 1.57079632679.
    -->
    <key qpos="1.57079632679"/>
  </keyframe>
</mujoco>

<mujoco model="pendulum">
  <compiler angle="radian" coordinate="local" inertiafromgeom="true"/>
  <option gravity="0 -9.81 0" integrator="RK4"/>

  <worldbody>
    <light diffuse=".5 .5 .5" pos="0 0 -3" dir="0 0 1"/>
    
    <body name="pendulum" pos="0 1 0">
      <joint name="hinge" type="hinge" type="hinge" axis="0 0 1" damping="0" friction="0"/>
      <!-- Massless rod: length 0.2485m -->
      <geom name="rod" type="capsule" fromto="0 0 0 0.2485 0 0" size="0.001" mass="0" contype="0" conaffinity="0"/>
      <body name="bob" pos="0.2485 0 0">
        <!-- Bob: sphere, diameter 0.1m (radius 0.05m), mass 1.0kg -->
        <geom name="bob_geom" type="sphere" size="0.05" mass="1.0"/>
      </body>
    </body>
  </worldbody>

  <keyframe>
    <key qpos="1.57079632679"/>
  </keyframe>
</mujoco>

<mujoco model="pendulum">
  <compiler angle="radian" coordinate="local" inertiafromgeom="true"/>
  <option gravity="0 -9.81 0" integrator="RK4"/>

  <worldbody>
    <light diffuse=".5 .5 .5" pos="0 0 -3" dir="0 0 1"/>
    
    <body name="pendulum" pos="0 1 0">
      <joint name="hinge" type="hinge" axis="0 0 1" damping="0" friction="0"/>
      <!-- Massless rod: length 0.2485m -->
      <geom name="rod" type="capsule" fromto="0 0 0 0.2485 0 0" size="0.001" mass="0" contype="0" conaffinity="0"/>
      <body name="bob" pos="0.2485 0 0">
        <!-- Bob: sphere, diameter 0.1m (radius 0.05m), mass 1.0kg -->
        <geom name="bob_geom" type="sphere" size="0.05" mass="1.0"/>
      </body>
    </body>
  </worldbody>

  <keyframe>
    <key qpos="1.57079632679"/>
  </keyframe>
</mujoco>

I have created the `pendulum.xml` file based on the specifications in `ChatGPT-5.3-Instant_Pend.YAML`.

```xml
<mujoco model="pendulum">
  <compiler angle="radian" coordinate="local" inertiafromgeom="true"/>
  <option gravity="0 -9.81 0" integrator="RK4"/>

  <worldbody>
    <light diffuse=".5 .5 .5" pos="0 0 -3" dir="0 0 1"/>
    
    <body name="pendulum" pos="0 1 0">
      <joint name="hinge" type="hinge" axis="0 0 1" damping="0" friction="0"/>
      <!-- Massless rod: length 0.2485m -->
      <geom name="rod" type="capsule" fromto="0 0 0 0.2485 0 0" size="0.001" mass="0" contype="0" conaffinity="0"/>
      <body name="bob" pos="0.2485 0 0">
        <!-- Bob: sphere, diameter 0.1m (radius 0.05m), mass 1.0kg -->
        <geom name="bob_geom" type="sphere" size="0.05" mass="1.0"/>
      </body>
    </body>
  </worldbody>

  <keyframe>
    <key qpos="1.57079632679"/>
  </keyframe>
</mujoco>
```
